content: Hey, I'm so excited for my trip to Austin next month. I've been reading a lot of travel blogs to get some inspiration for my own blog.
That sounds like a great trip! What kind of things are you looking to do or see in Austin?
I'm really into food, so I'm planning to try some of the best Thai restaurants in the city. I've also been painting a lot lately, so I might check out some local art scenes.
Austin has a great food scene. Have you tried any good Thai recipes lately that you're looking to try in Austin?
Actually, I've been experimenting with a new recipe for green curry. I actually learned how to make it from my colleague, Rachel, who's from Thailand.
That sounds delicious. Rachel must be a great resource for learning about Thai culture. What do you like to do in your free time besides cooking and painting?
I also enjoy urban sketching and taking care of my vintage car. It's a 1969 Mustang that I've had since I was 20.
That's really cool. It's great that you have so many hobbies. Do you find that your work as a real estate agent in Denver gives you a lot of inspiration for your art?
Yeah, I do. I get to see a lot of different neighborhoods and buildings, which is really interesting for someone who loves architecture and art.
That makes sense. It's great that you can combine your work and hobbies. Are you planning on moving to Austin anytime soon or is Denver still your home base?

For now, Denver is still my home base, but I do have to take care of my aging parents, so I might be making some changes in the future. My parents live in a small town about an hour outside of Denver.
It can be tough to balance work and family responsibilities. I hope everything works out for you and your parents. Do you have any fun plans for the rest of the year?
Yeah, I'm actually planning a trip to Thailand in December to visit Rachel's family and get some more inspiration for my cooking and art.
